authors: Jing Meng Haiyan Wang a School of Media and Communication
raw_title: Podcasting as Alternative Media: Navigating Politics, Platform Power, and Journalistic Professionalism in China
title_en: Podcasting as Alternative Media: Navigating Politics, Platform Power, and Journalistic Professionalism in China
title_zh: 播客作为替代媒体：在中国应对政治、平台权力与新闻专业主义
plain_summary_zh: 这篇研究探讨了在中国，播客如何作为一种替代媒体形式运作。它分析了播客创作者如何在政治限制、大型平台的控制以及新闻专业标准之间找到平衡。研究关注播客如何提供不同于主流媒体的内容，同时应对各种挑战。
plain_summary_en: This research examines how podcasting functions as an alternative media form in China. It analyzes how podcast creators navigate between political constraints, the power of large platforms, and journalistic standards. The study focuses on how podcasts offer content different from mainstream media while facing various challenges.
research_direction_zh: 数字媒体环境下的替代媒体研究，特别关注非主流媒体形式在威权政治和平台资本主义背景下的生存策略与内容创新。
research_direction_en: Research on alternative media in digital environments, particularly focusing on survival strategies and content innovation of non-mainstream media forms under authoritarian politics and platform capitalism.
